summary: Stancil argues that in today’s AI-driven data world, the future isn’t legible: multiple plausible product paths exist, but which one wins will be decided by accidental breakthroughs rather than careful planning. Because the “music” of predictable market patterns is gone, the only viable strategy is to build and iterate—start typing, then see what the market does.
money_quotes: Because if there is a single story that explains how AI changes the world, it is that it happens by accident.
Almost certainly, whatever changes happen in the data world over the next five years will also happen because of similar accidents.
There is no such thing as a long term plan.
Generational riches, it turns out, also starts with typing, not thinking.
But now, more than ever, it seems like the only way to find them—it is to start typing.
key_insight: In an AI-disrupted data economy where outcomes hinge on accidents and viral breakouts, the only reliable move is to build first and adapt fast.
